mentions.note: Down 6.6% after hours; not mentioned in new-high list, indicating market skepticism despite data center tailwinds.
Down 6.6% after close. Not on new highs list; facing headwinds from broader AI/data center rotation.
Got into data center business years ago to avoid AI capex trap. Results Wednesday will reveal if strategy is working; mixed outlook on feasibility.
Data center builder receiving Computex shout-out as infrastructure winner. Benefits from sovereign AI and hyperscaler buildout capex cycle.
Data center builder and cloud infrastructure provider. Down 40% from highs but strong quarter. Stock being bet against is bummer bet—should own it.
Surged on building custom data centers; critical infrastructure for hyperscalers deploying AI.
Up 13% from $145 (near $345 entry into data center business); parts disruptable, parts not; oversold bounce but structural exposure to AI disruption unclear.
Difficult to value; big data center spend timing questioned; harder to own than competitors; wait for June earnings; Core Weave more attractive.
Enterprise software company down 3.7%; caught in software selloff despite legacy strength; sector rotation away from traditional software.
Good company but others better positioned; not preferred entry in current environment.

Q3 crushed fears with 22% revenue growth, 21% EPS beat, $553B RPO. Capex-light data center strategy (customer prepayments/GPU supply) eases balance sheet concerns. $30B debt raise complete; largest customer OpenAI stabilizing with $110B funding.
Wait to see how Oracle acts to find out about data centers; king of the data center with fastest growth; must prove demand extends beyond hyperscalers.
Most important earnings this week; must prove data center buildout profitable; stock halved from $345 peak; Open AI/Abilene facility rumors need clarity before committing.
Caller (Ahab) from 288 asking to hold; don't like what they're doing to balance sheet; not investable right now; may bounce but should sell on bounce.
Enterprise software provider down 3% in broader sector selloff; AI displacement risk weighing on valuation despite solid business.
Raising $45–50B for aggressive data center buildout; bond/equity deal wildly oversubscribed; capacity expansion supporting memory and storage demand.
Receiving hundreds of billions in OpenAI data center orders; directly exposed to OpenAI's funding success and revenue growth.

Caller Gary concerned about bottom falling out; debt-funded data-center buildout, OpenAI client solvency concerns now table-off; 26x earnings; Cramer won't buy due to unclear business model but removes downside risk if weakness continues.
Up 93%, revenue +21%, massive debt load alongside CoreWeave; fine for leverage play but highly leveraged position.
